name: Knowth
description: Knowth, one of the principal sites of the Brú na Bóinne complex in County Meath, Ireland, is a Neolithic passage grave of immense archaeological and mythological significance. Older than the Egyptian pyramids, it predates 3200 BC, marking it as a central location for early Irish cultures. The mound itself is vast, containing two passage tombs placed back-to-back, one opening to the east and the other to the west. It is surrounded by 127 smaller passage tombs, satellite mounds that add to the complexity and mystery of the site. Knowth is not merely a burial ground; it is believed to be a symbolic representation of the cosmos, a place where the living could connect with the ancestors and the otherworld. Mythologically, Knowth is associated with the Tuatha Dé Danann, the gods and goddesses of Irish lore. Legends suggest that powerful figures like Nuada Airgetlám, the first king of the Tuatha Dé Danann, may have been buried here. It is also connected to stories of magic, druidic practices, and the cycles of life, death, and rebirth. The intricate carvings found within the passage tombs, including depictions of lunar maps and astronomical symbols, suggest a deep understanding of the heavens and their influence on the earth. Knowth remains a powerful symbol of Ireland's ancient past, a place where history and mythology intertwine, offering insights into the beliefs and practices of the people who built it.
subtitle: Ancient passage tomb of immense significance

keyMyths.title: Burial of Nuada
keyMyths.description: Legend states that Nuada, the king of the Tuatha Dé Danann, might be buried within Knowth, making it a place of royal and divine significance. It connects the site directly to the powerful gods of Irish mythology.

keyMyths.title: Astronomical Alignments
keyMyths.description: The passage tombs within Knowth are aligned with specific astronomical events, suggesting a deep understanding of the cosmos and a connection to the cycles of the sun and moon. This alignment reinforces the site's spiritual and ritualistic importance.

sources.author: Michael J. O'Kelly
sources.work: Early Ireland: An Introduction to Irish Prehistory
sources.description: Provides an overview of Irish prehistory and the significance of sites like Knowth.
sources.text: Provides an overview of Irish prehistory and the significance of sites like Knowth.

sources.author: Peter Harbison
sources.work: Pre-Christian Ireland: From the First Settlers to the Early Celts
